news.t: Here are some ETFs State Street likes with recession worries, Fed rate hikes in focus
ETF flows point to ‘skittish' investors as it's ‘a little unwieldy out there' after Fed rate hikes
3 Charts That Suggest Global Equities Are Headed Lower

holdings.infoBox: SPDW is an equity ETF with a total of 2481 individual holdings. The top holdings are Samsung Electronics stock at 3.17%, SK hynix at 2.65%, ASML Holding at 2.16%, HSBC Holdings at 0.96%, and Novartis AG at 0.86%.

overview.description: The State Street SPDR Portfolio Developed World ex-US ETF (SPDW) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the S&P Developed x United States BMI index. The fund tracks the performance of a market-cap-weighted index of developed stocks located outside of the United States. SPDW was launched on Apr 20, 2007 and is issued by State Street.
infoTable: S&P Developed x United States BMI

data.text: On charts of ETFs used to track global financial markets, nearby resistance suggests that momentum could turn in favor of the bears.
